Let $S$ be a set with an asymmetric relation $<$; this means that if $a, b \in
S$ and $a < b$, then we do not have $b < a$. Prove that there exists a set $T$
containing $S$ with an asymmetric relation $\prec$ with the property that if
$a, b \in S$, then $a < b$ if and only if $a \prec b$, and if $x, y \in T$ with
$x \prec y$, then there exists $t \in T$ such that $x \prec t \prec y$ ($t \in
T$ means "$t$ is an element of $T$").
